title: Add FrameStart/FrameEnd range control and read-only frame properties to SKLottieView
body:
`SKLottieView` had no programmatic playback control beyond setting `Progress` directly, making stateful animations (toggle switches, multi-state icons) require polling hacks.

## New read-only bindable properties

- `Fps` — animation frame rate
- `FrameCount` — frames in the active range
- `CurrentFrame` — current frame, zero-based within the active range, updated every frame

## Frame range control: `FrameStart` / `FrameEnd`

Two settable `BindableProperty` integers that restrict playback to a sub-range of the InPoint→OutPoint span. `Duration`, `FrameCount`, `Progress`, and `CurrentFrame` operate transparently within the active range. Respected in both forward and reverse playback directions.

| Property | Default | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `FrameStart` | `0` | First frame to play (0-based from InPoint) |
| `FrameEnd` | `-1` | End frame, exclusive (`-1` = OutPoint, same convention as `RepeatCount = -1`) |

```csharp
// Multi-state animation: each state is a different frame range
lottieView.FrameStart = 0;  lottieView.FrameEnd = 30;  // idle
lottieView.FrameStart = 30; lottieView.FrameEnd = 50;  // pressed
lottieView.FrameStart = 0;  lottieView.FrameEnd = -1;  // full animation
```

Values are clamped on assignment and persist across source changes (clamped to the new animation's frame count). XAML two-way binding works directly.

## Other

- InPoint/OutPoint from the Lottie file are always respected by Skottie; `FrameStart`/`FrameEnd` further sub-clip that range
- 14 unit tests covering frame range behaviour
